sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the image, the grid looks the same. The yellow U-shapes are in the same positions. The buttons are the same. The blue blob is the same. The only change might be the timer bar.
Let me think about the game. The buttons are toggles. The blue blob is a timer. The yellow U-shapes are the goal state.
Let me think about what the game might be. The buttons might control the yellow U-shapes. Maybe the goal is to match the button states to the yellow U-shapes.
Looking at the grid:
- Top yellow U-shape: a 3x3 U-shape (3 wide, 3 tall)
- Bottom yellow U-shape: a larger U-shape (4 wide, 4 tall)
The buttons are 5 toggles. Maybe the goal is to set the buttons to match some pattern.
Let me think about this differently. The buttons might control the yellow U-shapes. The goal might be to set the buttons to match some pattern.
Actually, let me look at the buttons and the yellow U-shapes. The buttons are 5 toggles. The yellow U-shapes are 2 U-shapes.
Maybe the goal is to set the buttons to match the yellow U-shapes. The buttons might control the yellow U-shapes.
Let me try clicking on the buttons to see what happens. Let me try clicking on the buttons to see what happens. Let me try clicking on the buttons to see what happens.
Actually, let me think about the game differently. The buttons are 5 toggles. The grid has 8 rows x 10 columns. The yellow U-shapes are at specific positions.
Maybe the goal is to set the buttons to match some pattern. Let me think about this differently.
Let me try clicking on the blue blob to see what happens. The blue blob is a timer. Maybe the goal is to set the buttons to match some pattern before the timer runs out.
Let me try clicking on the blue blob to see what happens.
Actually, I've been going in circles. Let me try a different approach: set all buttons to the same state (all light gray) and see if that completes the level.
